summary: Patients are generally willing to participate in community-based medical education if it includes collaborative design, mutual benefit, and respects local culture and values—an approach consistently shown successful across diverse underserved groups worldwide. These principles directly apply to regional and rural Australia given their emphasis on reciprocity, trust, and shared control within healthcare initiatives.
